description: Core programming language for ML/AI development. Used for data processing, model training, and API development.
Vectors, matrices, eigenvalues, SVD. Core mathematical foundation for understanding neural networks and ML algorithms.
Foundational knowledge: distributions, hypothesis testing, Bayesian reasoning, confidence intervals. Essential for model evaluation.
Numerical computing library for Python. Provides n-dimensional array operations, linear algebra, and Fourier transforms.
Data manipulation and analysis library. Used for loading, cleaning, transforming, and analysing structured data.
Machine learning library for classical ML algorithms: classification, regression, clustering, dimensionality reduction.
Open-source deep learning framework for building and training neural networks. Industry standard for research and production ML.
Transforming raw data into meaningful features for ML models. Includes encoding, normalisation, imputation, and domain-specific transformations.
Selecting and interpreting evaluation metrics: accuracy, precision, recall, F1, AUC-ROC, BLEU, RMSE. Cross-validation strategies.
Containerisation platform for packaging applications and dependencies. Required for reproducible ML environments and deployment.
Container orchestration system for automating deployment, scaling, and management of containerised applications in production.
Practices for deploying, monitoring, and maintaining ML models in production: CI/CD for ML, model versioning, data pipelines.
Tracking ML experiments, parameters, metrics, and model artefacts. MLflow is the open-source standard for experiment management.
Framework for building LLM-powered applications and agents. Used for orchestrating multi-step reasoning and RAG pipelines.
Databases designed for storing and querying high-dimensional embedding vectors. Used for semantic search and RAG systems. Examples: Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector.
Building and interpreting statistical models: regression, classification, clustering. Understanding model assumptions and limitations.
Supervised and unsupervised algorithms: decision trees, SVMs, ensemble methods, clustering, dimensionality reduction.
Neural network architectures, backpropagation, optimisers, regularisation. CNNs, RNNs, transformers overview.
Translating analytical findings into clear narratives for non-technical stakeholders. Visualisation design principles.
Python with Pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, and Jupyter for end-to-end data science workflows.
Querying relational databases to extract, join, and aggregate datasets for analysis and modelling.
Profiling datasets: distributions, correlations, outliers, missing data. Forming hypotheses before modelling.
Designing controlled experiments, computing significance, avoiding common pitfalls in causal inference.
Building clear charts with Matplotlib, Seaborn, or Plotly. Choosing the right chart for the question.
Creating predictive features from raw data: encoding, scaling, aggregation, time-based and domain features.
Running data science workloads on cloud platforms: managed notebooks, scalable compute, and dataset storage.
Primary tool for data extraction, transformation, and analysis in relational databases.
Advanced spreadsheet skills: pivot tables, VLOOKUP, array formulas, data validation, and macro automation.
Using Python (Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Seaborn) for data wrangling, exploration, and visualisation.
A/B testing, t-tests, chi-square, p-values, confidence intervals. Validating data-driven conclusions rigorously.
Business intelligence tools for building interactive dashboards and visual reports for stakeholders.
Handling missing values, deduplication, type coercion, and reshaping messy datasets into analysis-ready tables.
Turning analysis into clear narratives and recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.
Designing dashboards that surface the right metrics: layout, hierarchy, drill-downs, and refresh strategy.
Defining and interpreting business metrics: retention, conversion, funnel, cohort, and unit economics.
Understanding how data moves from sources to warehouses; scheduled extracts, transforms, and loads.
Structured Query Language for relational databases. DDL, DML, joins, aggregations, indexing, and query optimisation.
Designing and implementing RESTful APIs following HTTP standards, status codes, resource modelling, and versioning.
Advanced open-source relational database. JSONB, full-text search, transactions, indexing strategies, and extensions.
JWT, OAuth 2.0, session management, RBAC. Implementing secure auth in APIs and web applications.
Containerisation for consistent development and production environments. Building images, writing Dockerfiles, docker-compose.
In-memory data store used for caching, session management, pub/sub, and rate limiting in backend systems.
Asynchronous communication between services using message brokers. Producers, consumers, topics, and delivery guarantees.
Designing scalable distributed systems: load balancing, caching, message queues, database sharding, and CAP theorem trade-offs.
System administration, bash scripting, process management, file permissions, cron jobs, and SSH.
TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP/S, load balancers, firewalls, VPCs, and subnetting. Essential for cloud infrastructure and debugging.
Building continuous integration and delivery pipelines using GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or GitLab CI. Automated testing and deployment.
Provisioning and managing cloud infrastructure declaratively using Terraform. State management, modules, and remote backends.
Orchestrating containerised workloads at scale. Deployments, services, ingress, ConfigMaps, Helm charts.
Setting up metrics, logging, and tracing with tools like Prometheus, Grafana, and ELK stack. Alerting and incident response.
Containerisation platform for packaging and running applications consistently across environments.
Core cloud services: compute, storage, IAM, managed databases, and networking on a major cloud provider.
Branching strategies, pull requests, merge conflict resolution, and trunk-based development.
Semantic markup, the box model, Flexbox, Grid, and modern CSS for structuring and styling web pages.
Core language of the web: closures, promises, async/await, the event loop, and ES2020+ features.
Typed superset of JavaScript: interfaces, generics, discriminated unions. Catches errors before runtime.
Component-based UI library: hooks, state, effects, context. The dominant framework for modern web UIs.
Managing client state with Context, Redux, Zustand, or TanStack Query. Server-state vs UI-state separation.
Building layouts that adapt across devices using media queries, fluid grids, and mobile-first principles.
WCAG guidelines, semantic HTML, ARIA roles, keyboard navigation, and screen-reader-friendly interfaces.
Bundlers and dev servers (Vite, Webpack, esbuild): module resolution, code splitting, and asset pipelines.
DOM manipulation, events, fetch, storage, and rendering performance characteristics of the browser.
Unit and component testing with Jest and Testing Library; end-to-end testing with Playwright or Cypress.
Reducing bundle size, lazy loading, memoisation, and Core Web Vitals. Profiling render bottlenecks.
Consuming backend APIs from the client: data fetching, caching, loading and error states, optimistic updates.
Branching, pull requests, code review, and resolving merge conflicts in a team workflow.
Shared language across client and server: async patterns, typing, and modern ES features.
Component-based frontend UIs with hooks, routing, and state management for full-stack apps.
Server-side JavaScript runtime: building APIs with Express or Fastify, the event loop, and streams.
Designing resource-oriented APIs: HTTP verbs, status codes, pagination, versioning, and error contracts.
Modelling data, writing queries, joins, indexing, and transactions in PostgreSQL or MySQL.
Schema design, normalisation, migrations, and using ORMs like Prisma or SQLAlchemy safely.
Sessions, JWT, OAuth, and role-based access control across the frontend and backend boundary.
Branching strategies, pull requests, and collaborative review workflows.
Containerising frontend and backend services for consistent local and production environments.
Deploying full-stack apps to platforms like Vercel, Render, AWS, or Fly.io; environment config and secrets.
Testing across the stack: component tests, API tests, and integration tests with realistic fixtures.
Structuring an app's services, data flow, caching, and scaling considerations end to end.
Automating build, test, and deploy with GitHub Actions or similar for full-stack projects.
Planning and running interviews, surveys, and usability tests; synthesising findings into insights.
Translating strategy into a sequenced, outcome-oriented roadmap and communicating it to stakeholders.
Running sprints, backlog grooming, standups, and retrospectives; working effectively with engineering.
Querying product data directly to answer questions about usage, funnels, and retention.
Designing experiments, defining success metrics, and interpreting results to make product decisions.
Defining and tracking activation, retention, engagement, and revenue metrics; building metric trees.
Aligning engineering, design, leadership, and customers; managing expectations and trade-offs.
Sketching flows and low-fidelity prototypes in Figma to communicate product ideas quickly.
Documenting problem statements, requirements, success criteria, and edge cases clearly for the team.
Applying RICE, MoSCoW, Kano, and cost-of-delay to decide what to build next under constraints.
Defining vision, target segments, positioning, and a defensible path to product-market fit.
Understanding APIs, databases, and system constraints well enough to scope work with engineers.
Combining quantitative analytics with qualitative insight to make and defend product decisions.
